name: Ogi / Akamu / Pap
intro: Ogi is the Yoruba name for fermented corn porridge. In the east, it is also known as pap or akamu, while it is referred to as koko in the north (although the northern variation is usually made with millet and contains added spices). Unlike standard corn-based custard, it has a tangy taste due to the fermentation. It goes well with Akara (page 135), Moin Moin (page 156), or even Puﬀ Puﬀ (page 184).

ingredients.item: heap tablespoons ogi powder or paste
boiling hot water Sugar or syrup to taste

method: Place the ogi powder/paste in a mixing bowl and add the cold water. Mix until you have a smooth, lump-free, slightly loose paste. Pour in the hot water a little at the time, stirring the ogi simultaneously. The mixture should start to thicken quickly. Place in the microwave for 30 seconds to 1 minute for a thicker consistency.
Serve hot with the coconut milk poured over the top and with a light sprinkling of sugar or drizzle of syrup. You can also top your ogi with fresh or dried fruits if you wish.
